headline: Corning beats estimates but stock dips on Q2 guidance

headline: Spotify stock falls, Corning drops, Bed Bath & Beyond revenue growth
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including Spotify (SPOT) shares taking a hit after missing on guidance forecasts, Corning (GLW) slipping despite topping first quarter earnings estimates, and Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) posting its best quarterly revenue growth in five years.

headline: Corning Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Stock Is Tumbling.
summary: Corning  reported better-than-expected quarterly results but issued a middling outlook for the current quarter, halting the recent rally in optical communications stocks.  Corning stock was up 92% this year as of the close of trading Monday.  This optical communications segment, which has become its largest business, generated net sales of $1.85 billion in the first quarter, up 36% from a year ago.

headline: Corning faces weakness in non‑optical segments as data-center business booms
summary: Corning forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, as weakness in non-optical segments continues to pressure ‌business despite strong demand for data center products. Shares of the gorilla ‌glass maker fell more than 5% in early trading. Slower replacement cycles for electronics and cautious ​consumer spending amid persistent economic uncertainty have pressured Corning's business, offsetting gains in its optical communications segment.

content.title: Corning faces weakness in non‑optical segments as data-center business booms
content.summary: Corning forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, as weakness in non-optical segments continues to pressure ‌business despite strong demand for data center products. Shares of the gorilla ‌glass maker fell more than 5% in early trading. Slower replacement cycles for electronics and cautious ​consumer spending amid persistent economic uncertainty have pressured Corning's business, offsetting gains in its optical communications segment.

headline: Corning (NYSE:GLW) Misses Q1 CY2026 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops
summary: Glass and electronic component manufacturer Corning (NYSE:GLW) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 12.6% year on year to $4.14 billion. Next quarter’s revenue guidance of $4.6 billion underwhelmed, coming in 2.2% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.70 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Corning (NYSE:GLW) Misses Q1 CY2026 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops

Next quarter’s revenue guidance of $4.6 billion underwhelmed, coming in 2.2% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.70 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.
